message:
fix(tests): add serial test annotations to prevent global mock state races

Problem:
--------
Tests in hook.rs and transfer.rs that use the global mock transport system
were experiencing intermittent failures when run in parallel. The mock
system uses global state (via OnceLock and static variables) that can be
contaminated when multiple tests modify or read from it concurrently.

Symptoms included:
- Tests passing individually but failing when run together
- Non-deterministic test results depending on execution order
- Mock invocation counts being polluted across test boundaries

Solution:
---------
Added `#[serial(mock_global)]` attributes from the `serial_test` crate to
all tests that interact with the global mock transport system. This ensures
these tests run sequentially within their serial group, preventing race
conditions while still allowing other unrelated tests to run in parallel.

Affected tests in hook.rs:
- test_process_hook_remote_success_mocked
- test_force_local_allows_even_when_remote_available
- test_force_remote_bypasses_confidence_threshold
- test_process_hook_remote_sync_failure_allows
- test_process_hook_remote_nonzero_exit_denies
- test_cargo_test_remote_success
- test_cargo_test_remote_test_failures
- test_cargo_test_remote_build_failure
- test_cargo_test_with_filter
- test_cargo_test_with_test_threads
- test_cargo_test_signal_killed
- test_cargo_test_toolchain_fallback

Affected tests in transfer.rs:
- test_execute_remote_applies_env_allowlist

The serial_test import was also added to the test module's use statements
in both files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
